helpful: CVSG cut in the statpack (27.1% vs 3.0% grant) was the single most decision-relevant number
Provisioned petition and BIO full texts made the GVR-vs-plenary-vs-mootness structure legible without any PDF fetching
Web search to confirm the related state case (Baxter) was still pending was cheap and load-bearing
gaps: The committed statpack has no 'Segment base rate by salience band' section although the prompt tells predictors to prefer it; the CVSG cut had to substitute

fedcourts query returned 0 rows for citation-based lookups (Anderson/Burdick/Crawford) at ~110MB of ranged reads per call; a topic or QP-text similarity retrieval would have found comparable cert petitions
No provisioned text for the petitioner's cert-stage reply or the RNC respondents' brief in support, both of which are on the docket and grant-relevant
Statpack CVSG bucket is small (59 resolved) and carries no split of grant into plenary vs GVR, which matters for cells like this where GVR paths dominate
notes: Forward cell with an unusually informative docket (CFR then CVSG). The main forecasting uncertainty is external state litigation (Baxter), which no corpus surface tracks; a 'related pending litigation' hint in provisioning would help.
